title: AI-Assisted Development Changes Estimation Rules
insight: Traditional estimation models don't apply to AI-assisted development. Tasks that take 4-8 hours manually complete in 20-40 minutes with Claude Code. WBS estimates must use AI-adjusted multipliers.
detectionSignal: Estimates based on manual development rates applied to AI-assisted tasks; actuals consistently 50-70% below estimates
prevention: IF project uses AI-assisted development THEN apply 0.6-0.7x multiplier to traditional estimates and track velocity to calibrate

title: Bimodal Velocity Distribution
insight: AI-assisted development creates bimodal velocity: either very fast (working as expected) or debugging-slow (hitting context limits or generating errors). Little middle ground. Standard deviation of task duration is much higher than manual dev.
detectionSignal: Task completion times cluster at <30% or >200% of estimates with few tasks near 100%
prevention: IF using AI dev tools THEN plan for bimodal distribution — add 20% buffer for 'slow mode' tasks rather than averaging

title: AI Velocity Multiplier Validated at 1.4x
insight: Across 22 tasks and 3 sprints, actual delivery was 40% faster than AI-adjusted estimates (15.5 vs 18 hours). The effective multiplier is 0.6x of traditional estimates, or 0.86x of already-adjusted AI estimates.
detectionSignal: Consistent over-delivery across multiple sprints indicates estimates are still conservative
prevention: IF AI velocity data exists from prior sprints THEN use empirical multiplier rather than theoretical adjustment

title: No Contingency for AI Tool Failure Mode
insight: WBS assumes consistent AI-assisted velocity. If the AI tool hits context limits, generates persistent errors, or loses conversation state, tasks could take 3-5x longer. No contingency planned for this failure mode.
detectionSignal: Project plan has zero buffer for AI tool degradation; all estimates assume optimal AI performance
prevention: IF AI tool is critical path dependency THEN add 15% contingency specifically for tool failure/degradation scenarios

title: Estimation Optimism in Sprint Scope
insight: Sprint scope was ambitious for a single developer, even with AI assistance. Each sprint included 6-8 substantive tasks. The risk was mitigated by the fact that tasks were well-defined and independent.
detectionSignal: Sprint backlog contains >6 tasks for a single developer; tasks are individually small but collectively ambitious
prevention: IF single-developer sprint has >6 tasks THEN verify each task is independent and well-defined before committing

title: Traditional PM Metrics Still Apply at Micro Scale
insight: Earned Value tracking (CPI 1.16, SPI 1.0) proved useful even for a 2-day, single-developer project. The discipline of tracking planned vs actual validates the estimation approach and builds calibration data.
detectionSignal: EV metrics show meaningful variance even on small projects
prevention: N/A — positive finding. Continue tracking EV on micro-projects for calibration data.

title: Scope Discipline Eliminates Estimation Risk
insight: Zero change requests across 3 sprints. When scope is locked and sprints are short (1-2 days), the primary estimation risk (scope creep) is eliminated. This is the strongest predictor of on-time delivery.
detectionSignal: Zero CRs filed during execution; sprint scope unchanged from planning
prevention: IF sprint duration is <3 days THEN enforce hard scope freeze — add ideas to backlog only

title: Next.js App Router Selection
insight: App Router chosen for server components and file-based routing. Appropriate for a dashboard with mostly static data rendering.
detectionSignal: Framework selection aligned with requirements
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: JSON File Data Strategy
insight: JSON files chosen over database for MVP simplicity. Appropriate for read-only dashboard with <100 data entries. Will need migration path if data grows.
detectionSignal: Data storage approach matches current scale
prevention: IF data exceeds 1000 entries THEN evaluate database migration

title: Tailwind CSS v4 Design System
insight: Custom Tailwind theme configured via CSS @theme blocks to match portfolio site design tokens. Ensures visual consistency across Stan's client-facing properties.
detectionSignal: Design system alignment verified
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Vercel Deployment Target
insight: Vercel selected for zero-config Next.js deployment. Free tier sufficient for demo traffic. Three production deploys with zero failures. Build time ~12 seconds.
detectionSignal: Deployment platform aligned with framework
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: No Authentication for MVP
insight: Auth deliberately excluded from MVP scope. Dashboard shows non-sensitive analysis data. Will need auth before sharing client-specific results externally.
detectionSignal: Public dashboard contains project analysis data
prevention: IF dashboard will display client-confidential data THEN implement auth before sharing URL

title: TypeScript Strict Mode Catches Data Mismatches
insight: TypeScript caught a nullable runTime field mismatch between data (null for pending agents) and component props (string). Resolved in <2 minutes. Strict types prevent runtime errors.
detectionSignal: Build-time type error caught before deployment
prevention: N/A — positive finding. TypeScript strict mode working as intended.

title: Card-Based Layout for PMO Audiences
insight: Card-based layout chosen for information density and scanability. PMO leaders need to quickly assess project health at a glance. Validated through dogfooding.
detectionSignal: Layout pattern matches user needs
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Color-Coded Gate Status System
insight: Green/amber/red/navy color coding for gate statuses provides instant visual assessment. Consistent with traffic-light patterns familiar to PMO audiences.
detectionSignal: Color system aligned with PMO conventions
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Mobile Hamburger Overlap Issue
insight: At 375px, the fixed hamburger menu button overlaps the page title. Discovered during dogfooding. Fix: add top padding to main content area on mobile to account for the fixed button.
detectionSignal: Fixed-position element overlaps content at narrow viewport widths
prevention: IF using fixed-position navigation elements THEN add corresponding padding/margin to content area at same breakpoint

title: Dogfooding Reveals UX Gaps Testing Misses
insight: Using the dashboard as a real user (viewing Combo D's own data) revealed 2 issues that automated testing and manual QA missed: the hamburger overlap and stale project card data.
detectionSignal: Issues found by using the product for its intended purpose rather than testing specific features
prevention: IF building a dashboard or viewer THEN dogfood with real data before declaring MVP complete

title: Per-Project Folder Organization
insight: Data organized in /data/[project-id]/ folders with agents.json, knowledge-base.json, governance.json, assessment.json per project. Clean separation enables multi-project support.
detectionSignal: Data structure supports growth
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: localStorage for MVP State Management
insight: Governance status updates and context setup saved to localStorage. Sufficient for single-user demo but not for multi-user or persistent state across devices.
detectionSignal: State management uses browser-local storage only
prevention: IF multiple users need to see governance updates THEN migrate state to server-side storage

title: Deploy-Early-Deploy-Often Pattern
insight: Three production deployments in 2 days kept the feedback loop tight. Each sprint ended with a live deployment, enabling immediate dogfooding and validation.
detectionSignal: Multiple production deploys per day with zero failures
prevention: N/A — positive finding. Continue deploying per sprint.

title: Push-to-Deploy via GitHub Integration
insight: GitHub repo connected to Vercel for automatic deployments. Every push to main triggers a production build. Preview deployments available for branches.
detectionSignal: Deployment pipeline automated
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Scope Creep Risk Mitigated by Sprint Discipline
insight: Primary risk was adding features beyond MVP scope during development. Mitigated by hard sprint boundaries and clear deliverables. Zero CRs filed across 3 sprints.
detectionSignal: Feature ideas emerging during sprint; developer tempted to add 'just one more thing'
prevention: IF new feature idea emerges during sprint THEN add to backlog, do not interrupt current sprint

title: Stakeholder Review Not Yet Scheduled
insight: Dashboard is functionally complete but Stan has not yet reviewed it. The Co-Pilot recommends scheduling review this week while development momentum is high.
detectionSignal: Deliverable is complete but stakeholder review date not confirmed
prevention: IF deliverable is demo-ready THEN schedule stakeholder review within 5 business days

title: Demo Script Needed for Enterprise Audience
insight: The dashboard tells a story (CloudSync FAIL → governance gaps → 4 decisions needed) but a demo script is needed to guide enterprise prospects through this narrative effectively.
detectionSignal: Product is complete but no structured walkthrough exists for non-technical audience
prevention: IF product will be demoed to enterprise prospects THEN prepare a scripted walkthrough before first demo

title: Physical Device Testing Gap
insight: Mobile responsiveness tested via browser DevTools only. Physical iPad/iPhone testing not yet done. Touch interactions, scroll behavior, and font rendering may differ from simulation.
detectionSignal: Mobile testing done exclusively via desktop browser simulation; no physical device test recorded
prevention: IF product targets mobile/tablet users THEN require at least one physical device test before stakeholder demo

title: Build Passes as Quality Gate
insight: TypeScript strict mode + Next.js build process serves as the primary quality gate. Zero TypeScript errors, zero build failures across 3 sprints. Sufficient for MVP but not for production-grade testing.
detectionSignal: Build pass is the only automated quality check; no unit or integration tests
prevention: IF product moves beyond MVP THEN add Playwright e2e tests for critical user flows

title: Conditional GO Is the Norm for Internal Tools
insight: Most internal tools ship with conditions. A conditional GO with 3 manageable items (stakeholder review, demo script, device test) is a strong outcome. Waiting for perfection would delay value delivery.
detectionSignal: Gate assessment returns CONDITIONAL rather than FULL GO; conditions are all low-effort
prevention: N/A — positive finding. Ship with conditions, track resolution.

title: Dogfooding as Testing Strategy
insight: Using Combo D to display its own analysis results is the most effective testing strategy. It validates data structure, rendering, navigation, and real-world usability simultaneously.
detectionSignal: Product can consume its own output; doing so reveals issues not caught by other testing
prevention: IF building a tool that processes data THEN dogfood with the tool's own data as first test case

title: CloudSync Story Is the Demo Hook
insight: The CloudSync FAIL story (62.9%, Grade F, 4 governance gaps) is compelling because it shows the chain finding real problems. Leading a demo with this story — not feature walkthrough — will resonate with PMO leaders.
detectionSignal: Demo audience cares about outcomes, not features; product has a strong outcome story to tell
prevention: IF demoing to PMO audience THEN lead with the analysis story (CloudSync FAIL), not the feature list
